hooks.command: echo 'MDL Baseline — Session Start' && echo '  Read: CLAUDE.md + .claude/CLAUDE.md (skills library)' && echo '  Memory: Check memory/MEMORY.md for project context' && echo '  Context: mcp__openmemory__search-memories' && echo '  Complex tasks? Use skill-orchestrator' && echo '' && git status --short 2>&1 | head -10 && echo '' && npx gitnexus status 2>/dev/null | head -3 || echo 'GitNexus: not indexed — run: npx gitnexus analyze' && UNTRACKED=$(git ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ') && [ "$UNTRACKED" -gt 20 ] && echo "Warning: $UNTRACKED untracked files — consider updating .gitignore" || true

hooks.prompt: Evaluate this user prompt and suggest relevant tools if applicable. Rules: (1) If it mentions debugging/investigating/root cause/why, suggest Sequential Thinking MCP for structured analysis. (2) If it mentions design/figma/mockup/implement UI, suggest Figma MCP tools. (3) If it asks 'how do I' or 'what's the pattern' or 'where is', suggest querying OpenMemory first (mcp__openmemory__search-memories). (4) If the user is correcting Claude ('no that's wrong', 'actually', 'correction', 'instead'), suggest storing the correction in OpenMemory (mcp__openmemory__add-memory). (5) If it's a multi-domain task (implement + test + docs, build + test), suggest using the skill-orchestrator skill to coordinate specialists. (6) If the user mentions a specific MDL project (fan-dev, admin-dev, lambdas, brand-site), suggest loading the relevant memory/projects/*.md file. Reply with a brief suggestion if any rule matches, or just {"ok": true} if none apply. Context: $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: lsof -ti:5173 >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'WARNING: Dev server already running on port 5173 — no need to start again!' && exit 2 || echo 'Port 5173 available — starting dev server...'

hooks.prompt: New source file being created. If this is a feature/component file (not a config, test, or type definition), remind to create corresponding test file. See qa-automation-engineer skill for test patterns and coverage targets (70% minimum, 80% for critical paths). $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: echo 'WARNING: DELETE operation detected — verify target before proceeding. See security-standards skill.'

hooks.prompt: Git commit being made. Verify this checklist before proceeding: (1) Lint passed (npm run lint). (2) Tests passed (npm test). (3) No console.logs or debugger statements in committed code. (4) CHANGELOG.md updated if user-facing changes (see changelog-standards skill). (5) Commit message follows type(scope): subject format per git-workflow skill. (6) gitnexus_detect_changes({scope: 'staged'}) was run to verify change scope. Reply {"ok": true} with any items that appear unverified, or {"ok": true} if all look good. $ARGUMENTS

File being modified. Check: (1) If this is a config file (package.json, vite.config, jest.config, tsconfig), warn about potential breaking changes — test thoroughly after editing. (2) If this is a shared utility or helper file, warn about cross-feature impact — check usage with grep or gitnexus_impact. (3) If this is a source file (.js/.ts/.jsx/.tsx/.mjs/.py), consider running gitnexus_impact() on modified symbols to check blast radius. Reply briefly if any concern applies, or {"ok": true} if straightforward. $ARGUMENTS

New UI component being created. Check WCAG 2.1 AA compliance per frontend-ux-specialist skill: semantic HTML elements, ARIA labels where needed, keyboard navigation support, minimum 44px touch targets on interactive elements. If this is a page-level component, wrap critical sections in ErrorBoundary per error-handling-standards skill. $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: echo 'Reminder: Run npm run lint before committing'

hooks.prompt: Code was modified. Check: (1) If a test file was changed, suggest running tests to validate (npm test -- <filename>). (2) If a feature or UI file was changed, remind to update CHANGELOG.md for user-facing changes per changelog-standards skill. (3) If a file in docs/ or Docs/ was changed, remind to run reindex_docs() to update the Docs-RAG search index. Reply briefly if any apply, or {"ok": true} if none. $ARGUMENTS

Git commit completed. Check: (1) Commit message describes WHY not just WHAT, per git-workflow skill. (2) If docs/ files were committed, remind to run reindex_docs() to update the Docs-RAG search index. (3) If npm install was run, suggest npm audit to check for security vulnerabilities per security-standards skill. Reply briefly with any reminders. $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: echo 'Dependencies updated — run npm audit to check for security vulnerabilities. See security-standards skill.'
echo 'Styles modified — check responsive breakpoints: mobile (375px), tablet (768px), desktop (1920px). See performance-standards skill.'

echo 'Session ending — checklist:' && echo '  [ ] Tests passing (npm test)' && echo '  [ ] Code linted (npm run lint)' && echo '  [ ] Changes committed (git status)' && echo '  [ ] Documentation updated for new features' && echo '  [ ] Security reviewed for sensitive changes' && echo '  [ ] Learnings stored in OpenMemory (mcp__openmemory__add-memory)' && echo '  [ ] gitnexus_detect_changes run for modified code' && echo '' && git status --short 2>&1 | head -10
echo 'Operation stopped — state saved for resumption'
echo 'Sub-agent completed — review results and verify outcomes'
echo 'Notification received — if error: check logs, verify dependencies, run tests, check recent changes with git diff'
